title: Obione spinibractea
taxonomy: Obione spinibractea is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It belongs to the genus Obione, which is a group of plants native to South America. The classification of Obione spinibractea is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Caryophyllales, family Amaranthaceae, genus Obione, species O. spinibractea. The specific name "spinibractea" refers to the plant's distinctive spiny bracts. Obione spinibractea is likely closely related to other species within the genus, such as Obione caespitosa and Obione fendleri.

intro: Obione spinibractea, also known as the "Spinibracteate Obione", is a plant species belonging to the Amaranthaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and reducing inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Obione spinibractea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
